name: Boundary and Degenerate Inputs
description: Functions that process collections or accumulate values can receive empty inputs, zero-valued inputs, or single-element inputs. The code may execute without error but produce a degenerate result — a zero total, an empty output, a no-op that still mutates state. Also covers boundary exposure: when input crosses a trust or format boundary (e.g., internal tokens passed to external contexts, raw user input reaching privileged operations), the boundary itself is the degenerate case.

teachingExample.explanation: finalize_invoice(line_items) sums items and marks the invoice finalized. If line_items is empty, a zero-dollar invoice is finalized — masking upstream bugs. Similarly, a Bearer token included in an external-facing log or response crosses a trust boundary.
